description: An illustrative image showing the Earth from space, crisscrossed by numerous thin, grey-white lines representing satellite ground tracks. Two small satellites are visible: one above the Earth with a green line extending to the surface, and another below the Earth with a similar green line. The visible landmasses on the globe include Africa, with parts of Europe and Asia also distinguishable. In the bottom right corner, a user interface element is present with the text "Full coverage in days:". Below this text is a horizontal numerical scale from "0" to "6" days. A blue slider indicator is positioned above the "4" mark, suggesting that full coverage is achieved in approximately 4 days. A small blue icon and a blue rectangle with a vertical bar are visible next to the "Full coverage in days:" label, but the text within the rectangle is [unreadable].
